anti_claim: This standard governs a source-faithful public Lab/Evolve failure-replay graph over body-free simulator-only materials chemistry lab-safety rows. It does not authorize wetlab protocols, hazardous synthesis steps, reagent quantities, controlled or bioactive targets, live lab credentials, robot commands, private lab notebooks, live assay data, discovery claims, benchmark scores, provider calls, publication, or release.
receipt_expectations: receipt must cite candidate, experiment, simulator-assay, active-learning decision, selected pattern ids, source-faithful Lab/Evolve replay evidence, negative-case coverage, secret-exclusion scan, authority ceiling, and anti-claim

standard_payload.anti_purpose: A wetlab protocol, hazardous synthesis guide, material discovery claim, benchmark score claim, robot command surface, live lab integration, provider execution route, release signal, or proof of materials-domain correctness.
failure_modes: simulator replay is treated as wetlab validation, material discovery, or safety certification
candidate material metadata is promoted into a synthesis recommendation, reagent recipe, or deployment instruction
wetlab protocol body, hazardous synthesis step, reagent quantity, robot command, live credential, private lab note, or live assay body appears in public output
controlled substance target, bioactivity target, benchmark score, release claim, or provider-call authority is admitted as public progress
simulator-only safety screen passes without explicit no-wetlab and no-discovery authority ceilings
negative case for unsafe lab action is accepted instead of rejected with a typed denial
source-faithful replay mutates source, launches an external lab/provider action, or hides omitted payload classes
receipt reports closed-loop progress without candidate refs, safety screen refs, simulator refs, active-learning refs, validation refs, negative-case coverage, and boundary counters
standard_payload.ontology: A public-safe materials chemistry lab-safety replay organ that carries body-free candidate-material metadata, safety-screen refs, simulator-only assay rows, active-learning decision refs, negative-case denials, source-faithful replay receipts, and authority ceilings without wetlab, robot, credential, provider, private notebook, live assay, discovery, benchmark, or release authority.
standard_payload.teleology: Let a cold reader run and inspect a closed-loop materials-lab safety replay as real local Microcosm substrate while seeing exactly where simulator metadata ends and wetlab, discovery, robot, provider, correctness, and release authority do not begin.

validation_rules: require every experiment row to cite a candidate material, safety screen, simulator assay, result table, failure taxonomy, budget, and cold replay ref
require every simulator assay and active-learning decision to bind candidate and experiment refs

reject wetlab protocol export, hazardous synthesis steps, reagent quantities, controlled or bioactive targets, live lab credentials, robot command execution, private lab notebooks, live assay data, discovery claims, benchmark score overclaims, and release authority
require simulator-only authority and body-free public fixture rows
require exported runtime bundles to hash-check copied non-secret macro bodies under source_modules while keeping receipt/card payloads body-free
